note: Crafts of the Umbral Depths/Radiant Wastes Reach (dark_light axis). Umbracraft is the dark-pole tradition (the arts of the hidden, sight-without-light, what the light would destroy); Radiance the light-pole (revelation, the beam, the burning truth). Learned by traveling the Reach or training with Umbrals/Blazeborn. Radiant Plateau is a Valley-edge foothill of the light end; Prism Sight is folk-Radiance. Both drift the wielder toward their pole. Note: dark_light here is the MORAL/KNOWING sense as much as the literal — dark = concealed/harbored, light = revealed/exposed — which is why they can partly co-fire (a place both revealing and concealing). axes signed for engine compat. Authored by Aevi (PO), SNG-016.

abilities.name: Umbracraft
abilities.narrationHints: The dark that hides and the dark that harbors — sight without light, the unseen made ally, the sheltering of what the blaze would burn.
abilities.description: The dark-pole mastery: concealment, sight-without-light, and the harboring of what should not be exposed. Not evil — the necessary shadow.
abilities.notFor: Harming from the dark for its own sake (that's a corruption Umbrals themselves fear); revealing (that's Radiance); places with no shadow to work.

tree.name: The Seeing Dark
tree.grants: See truly in lightlessness; wrap yourself and a small space in concealment; find and use the shadows any place holds.
tree.cannot: Total light defeats it; concealment hides, it does not defend.

tree.name: The Harbor
tree.grants: Hide a person, a truth, or a thing so well even a searcher who knows it's there can't find it; move unseen through watched places; shelter what the light hunts.
tree.cannot: Harboring something makes you responsible for it; some things should not be harbored, and the Harbor doesn't judge — you must.

tree.name: The Deep Umbral
tree.grants: Command shadow at scale — hide a caravan, a rite, a rebellion; see into the hidden hearts of things and places; give sanctuary the Blaze cannot breach. The Underlight's power in miniature.
tree.cannot: The wielder drifts dark-hot — and the one who harbors everything eventually harbors the things that should have faced the light; darkness total is its own foreclosure, a world that never has to be seen.

abilities.name: Radiance
abilities.narrationHints: Light that reveals and light that burns — the hidden dragged into day, the lie shown its own shape, the beam that ends what can't bear seeing.
abilities.description: The light-pole mastery: revelation, exposure, and the burning beam. The full craft the Valley's Prism Sight only foreshadows.
abilities.notFor: Concealment (that's Umbracraft); mercy (Radiance shows, it does not spare); truths that should stay harbored — it cannot choose, only reveal.

tree.name: The Revealing Light
tree.grants: Throw light that shows things as they are — no illusion, no disguise, no shadow holds; a focused beam that can burn or blind.
tree.cannot: Reveals surfaces and shapes, not hearts; light spent blinds you too.

tree.name: The Unshadowed
tree.grants: Strip a place or person of every concealment at once; make a lie visibly a lie (unless it's built of true pieces — the Cathedral defeats this); a beam that ends a threat cleanly.
tree.cannot: The convincing-lie-of-true-pieces survives it; some truths, revealed, do more harm than the hiding did — and Radiance can't tell which.

tree.name: The Blaze
tree.grants: Revelation at scale — a whole deception undone, a hidden thing dragged into the light before a people, a beam that ends the unendurable. The Blaze's power in miniature.
tree.cannot: The wielder drifts light-hot — and total revelation burns its own land to glass; the one who reveals everything leaves nothing sheltered enough to live; light absolute is foreclosure by exposure, a world with no shadow left to grow in.

abilities.name: Darksight
abilities.narrationHints: The lightless place opens up clear as noon.
abilities.description: See truly in total darkness — the first gift of the Umbral road.
abilities.notFor: Seeing through concealment (that needs Radiance); light-blind.

tree.name: Darksight
tree.grants: See clearly in lightlessness; move sure through the dark.
tree.cannot: Total light defeats it; sees dark, not hidden intent.

tree.name: The Long Dark
tree.grants: See far and fine without light — read a face across a black room, follow a trail by the shape of the air in it.
tree.cannot: Sudden light still blinds you; you see the dark, not the intent moving through it.

tree.name: Nightborn
tree.grants: The dark becomes your native medium: you see better in it than most see at noon, and you can choose light-blindness and shed it at will.
tree.cannot: A life spent in the dark marks you — Blazeborn read it on sight, and do not love what they read.

abilities.name: Lightsense
abilities.narrationHints: Every source and shadow of light in a place, mapped at a glance.
abilities.description: Read light and shadow — where it falls, what it hides, how to use it.

tree.name: Lightsense
tree.grants: Read a place's light and shadow; know where you're seen and where you're not.
tree.cannot: Reads, doesn't make; the deep-hidden stay hidden.

tree.name: Spectrum-Read
tree.grants: Read light's whole spectrum — residual heat, the age of a scorch, the flush of a lie rising in a face.
tree.cannot: It reports what the light says, not what is true; a good liar with cold blood defeats it.

tree.name: The Unbroken Beam
tree.grants: Follow any beam back to its source across a valley, and know every surface it touched on the way.
tree.cannot: Darkness stays genuinely opaque — the gap the Umbrals built an entire art inside.

abilities.name: Dim
abilities.narrationHints: The light gutters where you wish it, and you're gone into the gap.
abilities.description: Pull shadow around yourself or a space — dim a lamp, deepen a shadow, slip from sight.
abilities.notFor: Total invisibility (that's the Harbor); lit-open ground.

tree.name: Dim
tree.grants: Deepen shadow and dim light to hide yourself or a small space briefly.
tree.cannot: Brief; bright open ground defeats it.

tree.name: Douse
tree.grants: Put a light out cleanly at distance — a lamp, a signal-fire, a Radiant's kindled beam mid-burn.
tree.cannot: You cannot douse the sun, and you cannot douse the Blaze. Dousing is always noticed.

tree.name: The Long Dusk
tree.grants: Dim a whole place — a hall, a street, a courtyard — down to working shadow, and hold it there.
tree.cannot: Holding costs continuously; a determined light-worker can push back against you, and will.

abilities.name: Kindle
abilities.narrationHints: A word and light springs up — to see by, or to sear.
abilities.description: Make and throw light — to illuminate, to signal, to blind or burn. Folk-Radiance.

tree.name: Kindle
tree.grants: Create light to see, signal, dazzle, or scorch.
tree.cannot: Ordinary light; spent, it blinds you too.

tree.name: Flare
tree.grants: Kindle a light that wounds — a struck brightness that burns what it lands on and leaves an afterimage seared in.
tree.cannot: Blunt. It announces you; nothing about a flare is subtle, and the dark remembers who lit it.

tree.name: The Struck Match
tree.grants: Light from nothing, anywhere within sight, at will — and it goes on burning after you let go.
tree.cannot: What you light you may not be able to put out. Ask the Blazeborn how that ends.

abilities.name: The Harbor
abilities.narrationHints: Hidden so well that a searcher who knows you're here still fails.
abilities.description: Hide a person, thing, or truth so thoroughly that even a knowing searcher can't find it — mid-tier Umbracraft.
abilities.notFor: Defending it (hides only); things that shouldn't be hidden.

tree.name: The Harbor
tree.grants: Conceal someone or something past even a determined, knowing search.
tree.cannot: You become responsible for what you harbor; the Blaze can still burn it out.

tree.name: The Deeper Harbor
tree.grants: Harbor a person, a truth, or a thing so completely that a searcher who KNOWS it is there still comes away empty.
tree.cannot: The harbored thing is now yours to answer for. The Harbor shelters; it does not judge — you must.

tree.name: Sanctuary
tree.grants: The harbor becomes a place: a warded shadow others can step into and be hidden within, and know they are safe.
tree.cannot: Everyone inside becomes your responsibility, including the ones you would not have chosen.

abilities.name: Unshadow
abilities.narrationHints: Every concealment in the place falls away at once.
abilities.description: Strip a place or person of all concealment and illusion in a burst of revealing light — mid-tier Radiance.
abilities.notFor: The true-pieces-false-whole (needs the True Word); mercy.

tree.name: Unshadow
tree.grants: Strip away disguise, shadow, and illusion from a target at once.
tree.cannot: The Cathedral's true-piece lie survives; some truths harm more revealed.

tree.name: The Stripping Light
tree.grants: Strip concealment from a place, not merely a thing — every shadow in a room gives up what it held.
tree.cannot: It reveals indiscriminately: yours, theirs, and things nobody wanted found.

tree.name: Nothing Hidden
tree.grants: Nothing within your reach can stay concealed — shroud, harbor, and false trail all fail at once in your presence.
tree.cannot: The Umbrals consider this an atrocity, and they are not entirely wrong. Some things were harbored for a reason.

abilities.name: The Harbored Flame
abilities.narrationHints: A sanctuary no one can find, full of healing light the dark would forbid.
abilities.description: The braid-craft: a concealed place filled with restoring radiance — shelter and healing woven from opposite poles.
abilities.notFor: Single-pole work; a searcher with their own light.

tree.name: The Harbored Flame
tree.grants: Make a hidden sanctuary of healing light — unfindable, and restoring within.
tree.cannot: The two poles fight; costly; own-light searchers can crack it.

abilities.name: Shroud
abilities.narrationHints: A lasting cloak of shadow laid over a place or path.
abilities.description: Lay a persistent concealment over an area — a hidden camp, a covered retreat, a path that resists being seen.
abilities.notFor: Hiding a single moving person mid-chase; total darkness banishment.

tree.name: Shroud
tree.grants: Conceal a place or route persistently so searchers pass it by.
tree.cannot: Bright light thins it; a knowing searcher with a lamp can work at it.

tree.name: Shroudwork
tree.grants: Shroud a thing rather than yourself — a body, a door, a whole wagon — so eyes slide off it entirely.
tree.cannot: A shroud is a lie of absence; touch, sound, and a dog's nose all still find what you hid.

tree.name: The Long Shroud
tree.grants: The shroud holds without your attention — set it and walk away; it keeps until something breaks it.
tree.cannot: What you shroud and forget, you may never find again. The Umbrals have lost cities this way.

abilities.name: False Trail
abilities.narrationHints: You leave the wrong story written in the dust behind you.
abilities.description: Lay a misleading trail — tracks, signs, and traces that send pursuers the wrong way.
abilities.notFor: Erasing all trace (that's harder); fooling a Verity-reader.

tree.name: False Trail
tree.grants: Send trackers and pursuers down a false path.
tree.cannot: A master tracker or truth-reader may catch the lie.

tree.name: The Braided Trail
tree.grants: Lay several trails at once, each equally convincing, and let a pursuer choose wrong.
tree.cannot: A tracker who knows your work will read the braid itself as a signature.

tree.name: The Trail That Was Never Walked
tree.grants: Lay a trail you did not walk — a path leading somewhere you have never been, convincing all the way to its end.
tree.cannot: It must end somewhere. What waits at the end of a trail you invented is not yours to control.

abilities.name: The Never-There
abilities.narrationHints: Not hidden — unhappened. You were never in the room; there is no trail because there was no one.
abilities.description: The stealth capstone: an infiltration so complete it leaves no trace, no memory, no gap — not concealment but erasure of your passage. The Umbral master's highest work.
abilities.notFor: Acting openly (any overt act breaks it); fooling a Verity-master mid-act; concealing others easily.

tree.name: The Never-There
tree.grants: Move through the most-watched place leaving no trace, memory, or evidence you were ever there.
tree.cannot: A single overt action ends it; a Verity-reader present may feel the absence; costs enormously to sustain.

abilities.name: Shadowstep
abilities.narrationHints: You step into one shadow and out of another, and the space between never had you in it.
abilities.description: Shadow-teleport: move between two shadows you can see, unseen in the crossing. The infiltrator's fold — shorter than Shortfold but silent and hidden.
abilities.notFor: Lit ground (needs shadow at both ends); long distance; overt arrival.

tree.name: Shadowstep
tree.grants: Step instantly between two shadows within sight, unseen in the passage.
tree.cannot: Both ends need real shadow; total light strands you; shorter range than an overt fold.

tree.name: The Stepping Dark
tree.grants: Step between shadows across a room, a street, a battlefield — anywhere dark touches dark.
tree.cannot: Both ends must be genuinely shadowed; a lit gap is a wall.

tree.name: Shadowroad
tree.grants: Travel the dark as a road — league upon league between distant shadows, arriving where no light has been.
tree.cannot: The shadowroad is not empty. Something else uses it, and the Umbrals do not speak about what.
